name: tryke-vscode
description: Tryke test runner integration for VS Code.

tryke.python.description: Path to the Python interpreter used to spawn worker processes. Passed to tryke as `--python` (requires tryke 0.0.26+). When unset, tryke falls back to `python` on Windows / `python3` on Unix from PATH — which usually isn't what you want unless your venv is already active in the spawning environment. Supports VS Code variables: `${workspaceFolder}`, `${workspaceFolder:NAME}`, `${userHome}`, `${env:VAR}`. Example: `${workspaceFolder}/.venv/bin/python3` (Unix) or `${workspaceFolder}/.venv/Scripts/python.exe` (Windows). Alternatively set `[tool.tryke] python` in pyproject.toml.

tryke.mode.description: Runner mode: 'direct' spawns a process per run, 'server' connects via TCP, 'auto' uses server if available.

tryke.server.autoStart.description: Automatically start the tryke server when using server mode.

tryke.server.autoStop.description: Automatically stop the tryke server when the extension deactivates.

tryke.server.logLevel.description: Log verbosity for the tryke server when the extension spawns it. Maps to `TRYKE_LOG=<level>` on the child env. On tryke versions that honor it (the release after 0.0.26), this surfaces both the rust runtime and the spawned python workers in the Tryke Server output panel; on earlier versions the env var is ignored. Applies to subsequent spawns; restart the server (Tryke: Restart Server) to pick up a change.

tryke.workers.description: Number of worker threads for parallel test execution. Null uses tryke's default.

tryke.failFast.description: Stop test execution after the first failure.

tryke.maxfail.description: Stop after N test failures. Null uses tryke's default (no limit).

tryke.dist.description: How tests are distributed across workers. Null uses tryke's default (test).

tryke.markers.description: Tag/marker filter expression (e.g. "slow and not network").

tryke.changed.description: Run only tests affected by git changes ('only'), run changed tests first then all ('first'), or disabled ('off').

tryke.baseBranch.description: Base branch for changed-test detection (e.g. "main"). Uses merge-base diff.

tryke.args.description: Additional arguments to pass to tryke.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run typecheck && npm run lint
